citations.narrative:
The facility failed to maintain some of their bird enclosures in good repair.
• The enclosure housing 2 amazon parrots has multiple areas of rust and flaking paint. The door and frame for the food
and water bowls have pitted surfaces with rust and flaking paint. The vertical bars and horizontal frame also have several
areas of rust with pitted surfaces. The rear metal walls of the enclosure have areas of corrosion resulting in holes at the
corners where the wall meets the floor. These holes are approximately 2 X 3 inches in size.
• In the aviary housing multiple parakeets, the wooden door frame has areas of decaying or rotting wood. The horizontal
brace for the door is a 2X4 that is broken and missing a portion where it crosses the diagonal brace. The diagonal brace
is also a 2X4 with a hole along most of its upper edge from rot and decay. These missing areas of wood have rough
edges with sharp protrusions.
Enclosures that are rusted, deteriorating, or have holes may pose a risk of injury to the birds and may compromise the
ability of the enclosure to securely contain the birds or restrict the entrance of other animals.
Housing facilities for birds must be kept in good repair, protect the birds from injury, and restrict other animals from
entering that may negatively affect the welfare of the birds within. To be corrected by March 25, 2026.

In the enclosure housing a rabbit, portions of the primary structural surfaces were observed to be damaged and worn,
creating rough edges. The wooden step/platform inside the enclosure had splintered and broken areas creating rough
edges. The wooden rabbit shelter had chewing damage around the entrance opening and edges, resulting in worn and
irregular surfaces.
Damaged enclosure components with rough or irregular edges may pose a risk of injury to the rabbit and do not maintain
the enclosure in good repair.
Primary enclosure shall be maintained in good repair to protect the rabbits from injury. To be corrected by March 25,
2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
3.150(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
The facility failed to maintain some of their bird enclosures in good repair. 
• The enclosure housing 2 amazon parrots has multiple areas of rust and flaking paint. The door and frame for the food 
and water bowls have pitted surfaces with rust and flaking paint. The vertical bars and horizontal frame also have several 
areas of rust with pitted surfaces. The rear metal walls of the enclosure have areas of corrosion resulting in holes at the 
corners where the wall meets the floor. These holes are approximately 2 X 3 inches in size. 
• In the aviary housing multiple parakeets, the wooden door frame has areas of decaying or rotting wood. The horizontal 
brace for the door is a 2X4 that is broken and missing a portion where it crosses the diagonal brace. The diagonal brace 
is also a 2X4 with a hole along most of its upper edge from rot and decay. These missing areas of wood have rough 
edges with sharp protrusions. 
 
Enclosures that are rusted, deteriorating, or have holes may pose a risk of injury to the birds and may compromise the 
ability of the enclosure to securely contain the birds or restrict the entrance of other animals. 
 
Housing facilities for birds must be kept in good repair, protect the birds from injury, and restrict other animals from 
entering that may negatively affect the welfare of the birds within. To be corrected by March 25, 2026. 
3.53(a)(1) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
In the enclosure housing a rabbit, portions of the primary structural surfaces were observed to be damaged and worn, 
creating rough edges. The wooden step/platform inside the enclosure had splintered and broken areas creating rough 
edges. The wooden rabbit shelter had chewing damage around the entrance opening and edges, resulting in worn and 
irregular surfaces. 
 
Damaged enclosure components with rough or irregular edges may pose a risk of injury to the rabbit and do not maintain 
the enclosure in good repair.  
 
Primary enclosure shall be maintained in good repair to protect the rabbits from injury. To be corrected by March 25, 
2026.

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
